steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Media Type Sniff Policy · Theory Media Type Sniff Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7282 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate upload byte and concurrency budgets before a quarantined state transition; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.

Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Media Type Sniff Policy model · Theory Media Type Sniff P…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: http.DetectContentType proves a file is harmless and correctly named. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
